name: Chicken Saute with Riesling
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces skinless boneless chicken breasts, cut into 8 equal pieces
1/4 cup (2 fluid ounces) dry Riesling wine
2 tablespoons minced fresh flat leaf parsley
1/2 teaspoon cornstarch dissolved in 1 tablespoon cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat 2 teaspoons of the margarine over medium-high heat.
2. Add chicken and cook for 2 minutes on each side until golden brown.
3. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.
4. In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 teaspoons margarine.
5. Add shallots and carrots to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, for 4-5 minutes until shallots are golden brown.
6. Stir in broth, wine, parsley, vinegar, thyme, chervil, salt, and pepper. Reduce heat to low.
7. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until carrots are tender.
8. Stir in the dissolved cornstarch and cook until sauce is slightly thickened.
9. Return chicken to the skillet and baste with pan juices, cooking an additional 3 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Each serving provides: 1 fat; 1 vegetable; 2 proteins
Per serving: 169 calories; 18g protein; 5g fat; 11g carbohydrate; 41 mg calcium; 248mg sodium; 41mg cholesterol; 1 g dietary fiber
